167081) Pera 29-2-2000 Br eearerne eet Ferry are MINISTRY OF RAILWAYS ~ (Railway Board) : NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 9th fuly, 2001 . « + t G.8.R. 513(E) — In exercise of the powers conferred by Sub-section (2) of Section 30 of the Railway Claims Tribunal Act, 1987 (54 of 1987), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend Railway Claims Tribunal Procedure) Rules, 1989, namely :— . 1 @ These: rules may be called the Railway Claims Tribunal (Procedure) Amendment, Rules, 2001. / Q) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2, tn the Railway Claims Tribunal (Procedure) Rules, 1989— (a) inrule 11, for sub-rule (3) the following sub-rule shall be substituted, namely :-—- “ (3) Hon scrutiny an application, is found defective and defect noticed is formal in nature, the Registrar may allow the applicant to rectify the same in his presence, and if the defect is not formal in nature, the Registrat may allow the applicant to rectify defect in a week's time, which may be extended in appropriate cases on the written request of the applicant.” (b) forrule 22, the following tule shail be substituted namely — “99 Summoning of witnesses and method of recording evidence (1) If an application is presented by any party to the proceedings for summoning of witnesses, the tribunal may issue summons for the appearance of such witnesses unless it considers that their appearance is not necessary for the just decision of the case, However, the reasons fot so doing will be recorded in writing. ” [No. 89/TC(RCT)/1-6 Pt Rajya Sabha] PADMAKSHI RAHEJA, Executive Director, Public Grievances Footnote :-—The Principal rules were published in the Gazette of India vide GSR 842 (E) dated 19-9-1989 and subsequently amended by GSR 700(E) dated 26-11-1991, GSR 438) dated 21-4-1992, GSR 509(E) dated 15-6-1994, GSR 270(E) dated 4-7-1996, GSR 59(B) dated 4-2-1997, GSR 719(E) dated: 15-10-1999 and GSR 167(E) dated 29-2-2000. .
